questions.question: Differentiate between anthropocentrism and biocentrism. (2 marks)
expectedPoints: Anthropocentrism is a human-centered view that attributes intrinsic value only to humans.
Biocentrism is a life-centered view that attributes intrinsic value to all living things.

questions.question: Discuss the principles of Deep Ecology and how it differs from a shallow ecological approach. (5 marks)
expectedPoints: Deep Ecology, founded by Arne Naess, asserts the intrinsic value of all non-human life.
It advocates for biocentric equality, meaning all species have an equal right to live and blossom.
It calls for a significant reduction in human population and interference.
It contrasts with 'Shallow Ecology', which is anthropocentric and focuses on resource conservation and pollution control primarily for the benefit of affluent humans.
Deep Ecology seeks a profound shift in human consciousness and identity towards nature.
